questions.question: What are Brighton & Hove Albion FC nicknamed?
Brighton beach is made of what instead of sand?
What's the name of Brighton's burnt-out, ruined pier?
Which monarch commissioned the Royal Pavilion?
Which area of Brighton is famous for its quirky, independent shops?
Brighton rock is what type of confection?
options: Boiled sugar stick with the town name written all the way through
A sherbet lolly shaped like a seagull

questions.question: ABBA won Eurovision at Brighton in 1974 with which song?
Which Graham Greene novel is set in Brighton's criminal underworld?
The world's first electric passenger railway opened in Brighton in which year?
What is the claim to fame of Brighton Sea Life centre?
An IRA bomb devastated which Brighton building during the 1984 Conservative Party Conference?
What was Brighton originally called in medieval times?
The 1979 film Quadrophenia — about mods fighting rockers — is set in which seaside town?
Who was the architect responsible for the Royal Pavilion's current exotic appearance?
What was Brighton & Hove Albion's original ground, which they left in 1997?
The famous 1964 Bank Holiday clashes on Brighton beach were between which two groups?
Which annual Brighton music festival is famous for breaking new artists?
Which area is Brighton's LGBTQ+ village?
Brighton & Hove was granted city status in which year?
What colours do Brighton & Hove Albion play in?
Who sponsored Brighton's current stadium, the Amex?
In which year did Brighton move into the Amex Stadium?
After leaving Goldstone Ground, where did Brighton groundshare while waiting for their new stadium?
Brighton Palace Pier opened in which year?
What was the Victorian-era Chain Pier primarily used for?
Devil's Dyke, north of Brighton, is best described as what?
options: A dramatic chalk valley with panoramic views

questions.question: Resident Brighton DJ Norman Cook is better known by what stage name?
Brighton is sometimes nicknamed what, reflecting its easy rail link?
Which National Park sits on Brighton's doorstep?
The Royal Pavilion's architectural style draws inspiration from which country?
Brighton's West Pier suffered catastrophic arson fires in which year?
Who managed Brighton to their first-ever Premier League season in 2017?
Which acclaimed musician is a long-time Brighton resident?
The Lanes in Brighton are built on the original street plan of which settlement?
options: The medieval fishing village of Brighthelmstone

questions.question: What does the old Sussex word 'laine' mean — as in North Laine?
options: An open strip of land used for cultivation

questions.question: The Royal Pavilion was purchased from the Crown and opened to the public in which year?
What is Magnus Volk's Railway, which still runs today?
options: The world's first electric passenger railway, running along Brighton seafront
A vintage steam railway to Devil's Dyke
A heritage tram through North Laine
A funicular up to the South Downs

questions.question: Kemp Town, Brighton's grand Regency estate, was developed by whom?
Brighton Dome, a major music and arts venue, is located inside which site?
Brighton's Old Steine was historically used by fishermen for what purpose?
Approximately how long is a fast direct train journey from Brighton to London Victoria?
Which famous seaside toy, popular with Victorian holidaymakers, was invented and first manufactured in Brighton?
options: The toy telescope / binoculars (Brighton pier telescope stalls)

questions.question: The Duke of York's Picturehouse in Brighton holds what distinction?
options: One of the oldest continuously operating cinemas in the UK
The first cinema to screen talkies in England
The only cinema inside a listed building

questions.question: Brighton's annual arts festival is one of the largest in England. Which Scottish city hosts the largest?
Preston Park is Brighton's largest public park. What is it particularly famous for hosting annually?
options: Brighton Pride — one of the UK's biggest Pride events

questions.question: What is the name of the distinctive observation tower that opened on Brighton's seafront in 2016?
Which Hollywood film was partly filmed on Brighton's Palace Pier?
The Brighton Fringe is the UK's second-largest arts fringe festival, after which city?
Brighton Marina, opened in 1978, was at the time one of the largest in Europe. What sits above it on the cliff?
options: The marina is overlooked by Roedean School

questions.question: What is the collective name for the stylish Regency-era seafront terraces in Brighton, inspired by Bath?
options: Regency squares and crescents — including Brunswick Square and Adelaide Crescent
